structural_claim: Phase transitions in quantum many-body systems reveal new states of matter with qualitatively different properties, where the collective behavior cannot be extrapolated from single-particle physics

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Nuclear matter undergoes phase transition to deconfined quark-gluon state with collective fluid behavior fundamentally different from hadronic matter
Periodic structures create forbidden bands where photon propagation is fundamentally altered, representing a phase transition in the optical properties of the medium
